note: round6 = DATA-SCALING WITHIN a single long clip (MAESTRO solo piano, ~12 min ≈ 700 chunks). audio_clips=1 fixed (same clip), --max-chunks scales the stream length WITHOUT changing data content -> the CLEAN amortization axis you wanted. Expect static_rate ~flat (same continuous source: every prefix has ~equal difficulty, no drift like the nsynth prefix problem) and online_rate to fall as the LoRA adapts to THIS clip -> delta = pure in-clip amortization. Frozen config (ti2/ep3/lr3e-4/r32-a64/c_attn/chunk_ms1000). VERIFY first with --max-chunks 64 (round-trip on MAESTRO's 44.1kHz stereo wav). max_chunks above a clip's real length just uses the whole clip. For a per-clip mean (avoid single-clip specialness), rerun pointed at other single clips.
